name: Healer
concept: Yours is the magic of the living body — to knit the torn, cool the fever, coax a failing heart to beat on. In a small tribe pressed by a hard world, that gift is the thin line between the Holt and the long silence. They cannot lose you, and every one of them knows it, and so do you.
duty: Your hands are the tribe's shield against the wounds of the hunt and the wasting of the cold. What you can heal, you must; what you cannot, you carry.

look.label: Look
options: marked as a healer of the tribe
plain hands that seem to glow when they work

look.label: Sign
options: hands that others reach for in pain
a way of touching a hurt before it's shown

gear.label: Kit
options: a healer's satchel of herbs and tools

gear.label: Keepsake
options: a lock from one you couldn't save
a remedy you've never had to use

moves.name: Healing Hands
moves.trigger: When you lay hands on a hurt and use the magic of the living body to mend it.

results.success: The wound closes, the fever breaks, the failing body steadies.
results.failure: The hurt is beyond your reach here, or the mending demands a price you must choose to pay or refuse.
advantage: Hold 1 Vigor (spend: draw out a poison or sickness as well / mend it without cost to yourself / rouse them to their feet, now / leave a lasting strength behind).
You learn exactly what harmed them, and what still threatens them.
threat: You take some of their hurt into yourself: a setback die until you rest.
The healing drains you deeply, or the hurt was hiding something worse.
results.triumph: You pull them back from the very edge, whole.
results.despair: The healing turns wrong, or they slip away despite you, and it marks you.

hold.name: Vigor
spend: Draw out a poison, sickness, or rot along with the wound.
Mend the hurt without taking any cost into yourself.
Rouse them to their feet and back into the moment, now.
Leave a lasting strength or resilience behind.

moves.name: The Tribe's Life
moves.trigger: Because your gift is the Holt's shield against death:
moves.text: The tribe protects you as they protect no one else; in a fight, elves will step between you and harm, and they will spend much to keep you safe. But you are precious and few — the tribe cannot afford to spend you rashly, and neither can you.

moves.name: Cleanse
moves.trigger: When you drive out a poison, a disease, or a wasting sickness.

results.success: The ill is drawn out; the body begins to right itself.
results.failure: It resists you, or the cure costs nearly as much as the sickness.
advantage: Cleanse several who share the sickness.
Learn the source before it spreads further.
threat: Some of it clings to you: a setback until you rest.
The cure buys time, not a cure.
results.triumph: You root the illness out entirely.
results.despair: You drive it out of one and into yourself, or it was never the true sickness at all.

moves.name: Steady the Mind
moves.trigger: When you ease a grief, a terror, or a madness in someone's heart.

results.success: The storm in them quiets enough to think and act.
results.failure: You cannot reach them, or reaching them, you take on some of their pain.
advantage: The calm lasts, and they trust you with what's beneath it.
You learn the root of their fear or grief.
threat: Their pain lingers in you afterward.
results.triumph: You help them truly heal, not just endure.
results.despair: You stir something in them, or in yourself, better left still.

moves.name: Herbcraft
moves.trigger: When you seek, prepare, or apply the plants and remedies of the wood.

results.success: You have or find the right herb, and know its use.
results.failure: It's out of season, out of reach, or you mistake one plant for another.
advantage: Enough to spare, or to store against later need.
You know a second, cleverer use for it.
threat: Gathering it took you somewhere dangerous.
The remedy has a hard side effect.
results.triumph: You find or brew a remedy of rare power.
results.despair: You misjudge a plant, and the cure becomes the harm.

bonds: ____ I have healed more times than I can count; their scars are half mine.
____ blames me for a death I could not prevent, and they are not entirely wrong.
I am teaching ____ the healing gift, and fear the losses it will lay on them.
____ guards me in every fight, and I have never properly thanked them.
There is a hurt in ____ I have not been able to mend, and it haunts me.
communityTie: The Hollow-Oak Holt lives because I can mend what the world tears; ____ makes sure I do not spend myself to nothing doing it.
When the hunt goes wrong, they carry the wounded to me first; ____ carries me when the weight is too much.
The tribe cannot lose me, and ____ is the one who reminds me I am more than my hands.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Vigor by 1.
Learn a deep healing: name a grave hurt (mortal wound, old curse, wasting) you can attempt to mend at Daunting difficulty.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Steel your heart: once per session, heal without taking any hurt into yourself.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Master the gift so wholly that even death's door does not always close against you.
Raise a place of healing: found a Bastion (an infirmary-grove) that draws the hurt of many tribes.
